result:
Done. All three missing operations are implemented in `CsvRow.calr`, the project builds cleanly, and behavior is verified — all 22 targeted checks plus a 5000-case randomized round-trip fuzz (fields drawn from commas, quotes, CR, LF) pass against the compiled assembly. It took 3 of the 10 build cycles (two Calor syntax fixes along the way: bare boolean variables in `§IF` can't be parenthesized, and list types must be written `List<str>` — `[str]` maps to `string[]`, not the pinned `List<string>`).

What was added, following the existing module's conventions:

- **`EscapeField`** (`§E{}` pure) — calls `NeedsQuoting`; if true, doubles internal quotes with `(replace ...)` and wraps in quotes, otherwise returns the field verbatim.
- **`JoinRow`** (`List<str> → str`, pure) — folds over the fields with `§EACH`, escaping each and inserting commas; the empty list yields `""`.
- **`SplitRow`** (`str → List<str>`, `§E{mut}` for the local `§PUSH`es) — a character-by-character `§WH` state machine with an in-quotes flag: doubled quotes inside a quoted field emit one literal quote, commas/CR/LF inside quotes are content, a comma outside quotes ends the field, and the final field (including the one implied by a trailing comma, or the single empty field of an empty line) is pushed after the loop.

`NeedsQuoting` is untouched, and the generated C# signatures match the pinned public surface exactly (`bool NeedsQuoting(string)`, `string EscapeField(string)`, `string JoinRow(List<string>)`, `List<string> SplitRow(string)` on static class `CsvRowModule`). The scratch verification project lived in `/tmp` and has been deleted; no test files were added and the `.csproj` was not modified.
